summary: Rule chunk from The Rules page 71, chunk 4.

mechanics.raw_text: I Polymorphism ‘The character may alter his appearance, and indeed his physical form, Mimicking another human being is easiest; anything which in- volves actually changing. bone structure is father difficult; and changing total mass is vir tually Impossible. Have the player state what frm he wishes to adopt, then make a power roll, Modify the power attribute for the difculty ofthe change; anything other than human appearance should iyea considerable stain. Ifthe rol succeeds, he character has achieved more or less the form he wants — the lower the rll, the closer he gets. If the, roll fails, so has the character = the higher the roll, the worse the failure. Exactly what happens in the event ofa failure {s up to you, Maybe he doesn’t change form at all, Maybe he changes all right, but to Something completely different from what he intended to become. Maybe he changes, and everything is fine — but when he tries to change back, he finds that he cant — the modifed form is now his “natural” one, “The mutant remains in his changed form for 1 40 5 minutes, after which he spontaneously reverts to “natural” form. I Precognition ‘The character has an imprecise and limited ability to see into the future, This usually takes the form of a conviction of the “rightness” or “yrongness” about a proposed action, or a sense of danger or reward about events in the immediate future. ‘Metaphysical Note: In order to preserve the GM's free will, we game designersmetaphysi- cal philosophers have arranged that Alpha Complex’s future not be rigidly determined. ral
